seoTitle: How to Connect Databricks to Power BI (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Connect Power BI to Azure or AWS Databricks using the native connector, Server Hostname, HTTP Path, and a Personal Access Token or OAuth.
intro: Querying big data lakehouse tables from Databricks doesn't require wrestling with ODBC drivers if you use Power BI's native connector. This guide covers gathering your cluster's connection details, authenticating securely, and choosing between Import and DirectQuery so you can browse Unity Catalog tables and build dashboards from Delta tables.

steps.heading: Get your cluster connection details
steps.body: In your Databricks workspace, open your SQL Warehouse or compute cluster, expand Advanced Options, and copy the Server Hostname and HTTP Path from the JDBC/ODBC tab.

steps.heading: Connect with the Azure Databricks connector
steps.body: In Power BI Desktop, click Get Data, select the Azure Databricks connector, and paste in the hostname and HTTP path.

steps.heading: Choose a connectivity mode
steps.body: Select either Import for scheduled data loads or DirectQuery for near real-time querying against the warehouse.

steps.heading: Authenticate securely
steps.body: Choose OAuth or a Personal Access Token, enter your credential, and click Connect to establish the session.

steps.heading: Browse Unity Catalog and publish
steps.body: Navigate catalogs, schemas, and Delta tables in the Navigator panel, load your target tables, and publish the report to a cloud workspace.

faq.question: Do I need to install a separate ODBC driver?
faq.answer: No, the native Azure Databricks connector in Power BI handles the connection without requiring manual driver installation.

faq.question: Should I choose Import or DirectQuery for large tables?
faq.answer: Import gives faster report performance for most use cases, while DirectQuery is better when you need sub-second reflection of live changes in massive datasets.

faq.question: Is a Personal Access Token safer than OAuth?
faq.answer: Both are secure options; OAuth is generally preferred for interactive user sessions, while tokens work well for scheduled or automated connections.

faq.question: Do I need an on-premises gateway to publish this report?
faq.answer: No, since Databricks is cloud-hosted, you can publish and refresh directly through the Power BI Service without a gateway.
